arts.ttl: Man City sent 100 points warning as Arsenal, Chelsea and Tottenham await Premier League decision
arts.sum: Fines, points deductions, and even expulsion from the league are the potential punishments the Premier League could impose should they be found guilty of the most significant alleged rule breaches. “If you look at the hearing for both Everton and Nottingham Forest, the commissions of both said, ‘this was a minor breach of the rules’, now if the allegations against Manchester City are proven, that surely has to be a major breach of the rules, effectively that the club misrepresented the money coming into the club.

arts.ttl: How Arsenal supporters can vote for Paul Merson on Strictly Come Dancing
arts.sum: Arsenal fans will soon be able to watch club legend Paul Merson on the dance floor as he takes part in this year's Strictly Come Dancing. Fans wishing to vote for Merson in the first week of Strictly Come Dancing will be able to do so on Saturday evening.

arts.ttl: Zinchenko new role, Gabriel Jesus gamble, Kai Havertz call - Arsenal North London Derby lineups - Kaya Kaynak - football.london
arts.sum: Kaynak's Arsenal XI vs Tottenham: Raya; White, Saliba, Gabriel, Timber; Partey, Zinchenko, Havertz; Saka, Jesus, Martinelli. Arsenal couldn't possibly legislate for Declan Rice's ridiculous red card against Brighton, or Gabriel falling on Mikel Merino in training, or Ousmane Dembele falling on Riccardo Calafiori in a freak accident, or Martin Odegaard rolling his ankle in the most inconspicuous of 50/50 challenges.

arts.ttl: Tottenham have Martin Odegaard injury 'suspicions' as Mikel Arteta mulls over big change
arts.sum: Tottenham aren't reportedly convinced Martin Odegaard's injury is all that serious despite the Arsenal man being pictured on crutches a few days ago. Rice, 25, is serving a one-match suspension after getting sent off during Arsenal's 1-1 draw with Brighton last month, while both Odegaard, 25, and Merino, 28, have picked up injuries.
